title: Creamy Dense Mexican Blackcurrant Horchata
description: A dense and earthy Mexican drink featuring blackcurrant jam, this horchata is a unique twist on the traditional Oaxacan beverage. It is a mediterranean-friendly drink that is perfect for warm weather.
instructions: Rinse the rice and soak it in water for at least 4 hours.
Drain and rinse the rice again, then add it to a blender with 4 cups of water.
Blend the rice and water mixture until it is smooth and creamy.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a large bowl.
Add the blackcurrant jam, cinnamon sticks, and vanilla extract to the bowl and stir until the jam is dissolved.
Let the mixture steep in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Strain the mixture again through a cheesecloth into a large pitcher.
Add the evaporated milk to the pitcher and stir until it is well combined.
Chill the horchata in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Serve the horchata cold, garnished with a sprinkle of cinnamon if desired.
